description_long: This object consists of three stacked cardboard parcels, each with a rectangular box shape and visible corrugated texture. The boxes are brown with black packing tape running along the tops and sides, and they feature various shipping symbols such as upward arrows and handling marks. The surfaces show signs of wear, including scuffs, dents, and small tears, suggesting they have been handled or transported. The arrangement is slightly irregular, with one box resting atop two others, creating a stable but non-uniform pile. The boxes are typical of those used for shipping or storage in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, in warehouses, or near loading docks. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements and a focus on function and durability.
description: A group of three worn brown cardboard parcels with black tape and shipping symbols, stacked in an irregular pile. The boxes show scuffs, dents, and handling marks, typical of urban shipping or storage.
description_view_0: Three brown cardboard boxes with black tape are stacked, with visible shipping symbols and wear marks; the top box is set back and to the left.
description_view_1: The largest box is most prominent, with its long side and black tape visible; the other two boxes are partially obscured behind and below.
description_view_2: The front view shows all three boxes clearly, with the black tape and shipping symbols most visible; the wear and tear on the surfaces is prominent.
description_view_3: The rightmost box is most visible, with its side and top showing black tape and handling marks; the other two boxes are partially behind.
